article_title: University of Houston Physicists Set New Ambient-Pressure Superconductivity Record at 151 Kelvin Using Pressure Quenching

content_preview.title: University of Houston Physicists Set New Ambient-Pressure Superconductivity Record at 151 Kelvin Using Pressure Quenching
content_preview.summary: Researchers use pressure quenching to lock in a record superconducting transition temperature of 151 K at ambient pressure, surpassing a 33-year-old record.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Physicists at the University of Houston have broken the long-standing record for the highest superconducting transition temperature achieved at ambient pressure, reaching 151 Kelvin (approximately minus 122 degrees Celsius). The result, published March 9, 2026 in the Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, surpasses the previous ambient-pressure record of 133 Kelvin that had stood since 1993, according to [The Quantum Insider](https://thequantuminsider.com/2026/03/11/univer...

recommendations: 1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured article with clear sections covering the technique, significance, companion research agenda, and future outlook. Appropriate technical depth for a News category piece at 704 words.
editor_notes.source_verification: Both sources verified. The Quantum Insider article confirms: 151 K record, Hg-1223 material, pressure quenching technique, Paul Ching-Wu Chu and Liangzi Deng as researchers, 133 K previous record from 1993, 8% grid electricity loss claim, PNAS publication on March 9, 2026, funding from Intellectual Ventures and State of Texas via TcSUH. Phys.org article (returned 403 to automated check but accessible via WebFetch) confirms: companion strategy paper by Christoph Heil of Graz University of Technology, 16-author team, two central challenges (prediction and engineering), stability for two weeks across five samples, 300,000 times atmospheric pressure.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims cross-checked against both sources. Key facts verified: 151 K temperature, 18-degree improvement over 133 K record, Hg-1223 material, pressure quenching at 300,000x atmospheric pressure, stability over 2 weeks and 5 samples, PNAS publication March 9 2026, companion paper by Heil et al., 8% grid loss figure, ~140°C gap to room temperature. No hallucinated claims detected.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism despite the breakthrough nature of the finding. Appropriately notes the 140-degree gap to room temperature, tempering expectations.
editor_notes.originality: No existing articles on superconductivity in the archive. Topic is entirely new.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality submission with solid sourcing, accurate claims, and balanced tone. Both sources independently confirm the key facts. Approved for publication.
